segments.text: The words of Agur the son of Jakeh, the revelation: the man says to Ithiel, to Ithiel and Ucal:
Surely I am the most ignorant man, and don’t have a man’s understanding.
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: I have not learned wisdom, neither do I have the knowledge of the Holy One.
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text:
Who has ascended up into heaven, and descended? Who has gathered the wind in his fists?
Who has bound the waters in his garment?
Who has established all the ends of the earth?
What is his name, and what is his son’s name, if you know?
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: “Every word of God is flawless. He is a shield to those who take refuge in him.
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: Don’t you add to his words, lest he reprove you, and you be found a liar.
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: “Two things I have asked of you. Don’t deny me before I die.
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text:
Remove far from me falsehood and lies. Give me neither poverty nor riches. 
Feed me with the food that is needful for me,
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text:
lest I be full, deny you, and say, ‘Who is Yahweh?’or lest I be poor, and steal, 
and so dishonor the name of my God.
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: “Don’t slander a servant to his master, lest he curse you, and you be held guilty.
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: There is a generation that curses their father, and doesn’t bless their mother.
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: There is a generation that is pure in their own eyes, yet are not washed from their filthiness.
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: There is a generation, oh how lofty are their eyes! Their eyelids are lifted up.
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text:
There is a generation whose teeth are like swords, and their jaws like knives, 
to devour the poor from the earth, and the needy from among men.
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text:
“The leech has two daughters:‘Give, give.’
“There are three things that are never satisfied; four that don’t say, ‘Enough!’:
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text:
Sheol, 
the barren womb, 
the earth that is not satisfied with water, 
and the fire that doesn’t say, ‘Enough!’
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text:
“The eye that mocks at his father, and scorns obedience to his mother, 
the ravens of the valley shall pick it out, 
the young eagles shall eat it.
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: “There are three things which are too amazing for me, four which I don’t understand:
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text:
The way of an eagle in the air, 
the way of a serpent on a rock, 
the way of a ship in the middle of the sea, 
and the way of a man with a maiden.
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text:
“So is the way of an adulterous woman: She eats and wipes her mouth, 
and says, ‘I have done nothing wrong.’
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: “For three things the earth trembles, and under four, it can’t bear up:
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text:
For a servant when he is king, 
a fool when he is filled with food,
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text:
for an unloved woman when she is married, 
and a servant who is heir to her mistress.
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: “There are four things which are little on the earth, but they are exceedingly wise:
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text:
The ants are not a strong people, 
yet they provide their food in the summer.
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text:
The hyraxes are but a feeble folk, 
yet make they their houses in the rocks.
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text:
The locusts have no king, 
yet they advance in ranks.
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text:
You can catch a lizard with your hands, 
yet it is in kings’ palaces.
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: “There are three things which are stately in their march, four which are stately in going:
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text:
The lion, which is mightiest among animals, 
and doesn’t turn away for any;
segments.speaker: Agur
Agur

segments.text: “If you have done foolishly in lifting up yourself, or if you have thought evil, put your hand over your mouth.
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text:
For as the churning of milk produces butter, 
and the wringing of the nose produces blood, 
so the forcing of wrath produces strife.
segments.speaker: Agur
